summary:
The concept of "high angle case" in the queried context pertains to fetal malposition, specifically involving aberrant head positioning within the maternal pelvis. No support exists in the provided evidence linking terms like FMP, FMIA, or SNA—commonly used in orthodontics—to obstetric high-angle presentations; thus, none of the offered options can be clinically justified based on available facts.  

(Note: This summary adheres strictly to the clinician-relevant point regarding accurate classification without relying on extraneous or unverified anatomical interpretations.)
